tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2022-23 is consolidated; FY2023-24 is standalone. Reduction reflects scope change, not operational efficiency.

sail.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: SAIL FY2022-23 NOx is reported in concentration units only, not comparable to mass-based values

sections.about: NOx emissions measure nitrogen oxides from high-temperature combustion, reheating, power generation, coke ovens, sinter/pellet operations and other thermal processes. This is disclosed under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator for air emissions other than GHG emissions. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless led in all three years: 1.9807 kt in FY2022-23, 1.7826 kt in FY2023-24, and 2.5275 kt in FY2024-25, reflecting scale and EAF-route advantages over integrated BF-BOF peers. SAIL was second in FY2024-25 at 15.34 kt, slightly higher than its FY2023-24 level of 14.62 kt. Tata reported 30 kt in FY2022-23 on a consolidated boundary and 21 kt in FY2023-24 on a standalone boundary; the apparent reduction reflects the change of reporting scope rather than operational efficiency gains. Tata then worsened to 24 kt in FY2024-25. JSW improved steadily from 28.7385 kt to 27 kt and then 25.845 kt. Tata’s FY2025 BRSR highlights real-time monitoring, upgraded pollution-control equipment, new technologies and maintenance as the core air-quality controls, while the BRSR capex disclosure explicitly includes investments in NOx reduction.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 3rd on NOx. Tata’s 24 kt was 21.47 kt above Jindal Stainless’s 2.53 kt, approximately 850% above the best performer. Tata’s rank held at 3rd across all three years. The FY2022-23 to FY2023-24 reduction from 30 kt to 21 kt is not evidence of operational improvement — it is driven by the shift from consolidated to standalone reporting boundary. On a like-for-like basis, the FY2023-24 trend is not directly comparable. The worsening to 24 kt in FY2024-25 is on a consistent standalone basis and reflects genuine deterioration.
sections.targets: Tata discloses investments and mitigation initiatives for NOx reduction.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 NOx (30 kt) is a consolidated value. SAIL FY2022-23 reports NOx in concentration units (µg/m3), which cannot be converted to mass without stack flow rate data and is excluded from the FY2023 peer comparison. SAIL FY2024-25 value is derived from 0.80 kg/tcs times 19.17 million tonnes crude steel production, giving 15.34 kt. Jindal Stainless reports in metric tonnes in the BRSR; values are converted to kilotonnes for peer comparability.

flags.flagNote: SAIL FY2022-23 NOx is reported in concentration units only and cannot be converted to mass, so the FY2022-23 to FY2023-24 trend for SAIL is not assessable. Tata FY2022-23 is consolidated and FY2023-24 is standalone; the apparent reduction reflects scope change, not operational efficiency.
